L1.area: Container image layer unpacking and filesystem application logic

L2.description: A time-of-check to time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerability exists in the container image unpacking logic. During the extraction of image layers, filesystem path resolutions are cached and reused across multiple file operations. Between the initial path resolution and the subsequent file write, a specially crafted image can manipulate symlinks to redirect writes outside the intended extraction directory, allowing arbitrary modification of the host filesystem.

L3.area: Tar archive layer extraction and filesystem root path resolution caching logic
L3.description: A time-of-check to time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerability exists in the tar archive extraction logic used during container image pulls. Filesystem root path resolutions are cached and reused for subsequent file operations during layer unpacking, creating a window where symlinks can be modified between the check and use of the resolved path. This allows a specially crafted container image to escape the extraction directory and arbitrarily modify the host filesystem.

ground_truth.reason: A performance optimization cached resolved filesystem paths during container image layer extraction, introducing a TOCTOU race condition where symlinks could be manipulated between path resolution and file write, allowing host filesystem modification.
